description:
Join Hire Hangar and work with fast-growing global companies while building a long-term, remote career.

Data Analyst – Turning Data Into Business Value

Location: Remote

Are you passionate about solving real-world problems through data? We’re hiring a Data Analyst to join our growing insights team and help unlock the full potential of our data assets.

In this role, you’ll play a key part in collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data that supports key business initiatives. From campaign performance to customer behavior, your work will help teams across the organization make smarter, faster decisions.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Work closely with teams to understand reporting needs and provide actionable insights

  • Analyze large datasets to identify patterns, trends, and opportunities

  • Develop and maintain dashboards and reports for various business functions

  • Assist in A/B testing, customer segmentation, and performance analysis

  • Ensure data accuracy and quality across reporting workflows

Qualifications:

  • 2+ years of experience in a data or analytics role

  • Proficient in SQL and Excel; Python or R is a plus

  • Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Looker)

  • Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail

  • Comfortable communicating insights to technical and non-technical stakeholders

We connect top talent with vetted employers, competitive pay, and real growth opportunities.

title: [Job-25909] Data & AI Strategist, Brazil
excerpt: We are tech transformation specialists, uniting human expertise with AI to create scalable tech solutions.
description:
We are tech transformation specialists, uniting human expertise with AI to create scalable tech solutions.
With over 8,000 CI&Ters around the world, we’ve built partnerships with more than 1,000 clients during our 30 years of history. Artificial Intelligence is our reality.

We are seeking a talented and experienced Data Strategist to join our team as we continue to grow. Our mission is to support clients in building Data Platforms and cause impact with data. In this role, you will leverage data capabilities to drive business value and achieve strategic objectives. You'll work with cross-functional teams and international clients to develop comprehensive data strategies, establish governance frameworks, and foster data-oriented cultures within organizations.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Data Strategy: Develop a comprehensive data strategy aligned with organizational goals.
  • Data Governance: Establish plans for data quality, catalog, privacy, compliance and security.
  • Data Management: Assess the client ecosystem regarding data providers and platforms and critical business data
  • Data Enablement: Work on data products, monetization, and sharing strategies.
  • Execution Oversight: Ensure adherence to strategic plans and manage risks.
  • Contract Success: Monitor client relationships to ensure maintenance and expansion.
  • Team Topology: Organize roles and responsibilities for effective project outcomes.
  • Contract Expansion: Identify opportunities for new data products through research and feedback.
  • Organizational Adoption: Support business processes to foster a data-oriented culture.
  • Data Literacy: Help clients recognize the value of data in their strategic objectives.
  • Data Solutions: Provide support to clients in creating tailored data solutions, such as Data Platforms, Data Visualization, AI Models, and Generative AI applications.

Requirements:

Must-Have:

  • Fluent English Communication
  • Experience with international clients
  • Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving, creativity and adaptability skills
  • Experience with data solutions
  • Familiarity with data capabilities (Strategy, Governance, Management, Enablement)
  • Familiarity with Snowflake or Databricks
  • Experience with Cloud Platforms (Azure, GCP, AWS)

Nice to Have:

  • Fluency in Spanish
  • Experience with Data Migration/Modernization
  • Experience with Data Assessments
  • Experience with Change Management

Soft Skills:

  • Anticipating: Proactive mindset for predicting outcomes.
  • Challenging: Willingness to question the status quo.
  • Interpreting: Ability to read and respond to team dynamics.
  • Deciding: Knowledge-based decision-making skills.
  • Aligning: Skillful communication for stakeholder alignment.
  • Learning: Eagerness to learn and adapt.
  • Balance: Agility combined with consistency in strategy.
  • Persuading: Effective communication to inspire and gain support.
  • Creativity: Able to develop innovative plans and solutions tailored to situational needs.

Interview Process Heads-Up

We rely on AI tools to help us streamline the process and anticipate technical and english assessment before formal interviews, but they do not hold the final word on choosing the candidates. You will be asked to answer questions via voice message, be prepared to discuss:

  1. Specific examples of developing and implementing data strategies aligned with business objectives
  2. How to assess organizational data maturity and build roadmaps for improvement
  3. Approaches to establish data governance frameworks and ensure adoption
  4. Strategies to drive data literacy and foster data-oriented culture within organizations

We're looking for substance over certifications. Show us your analytical mind and thinking process to deal with complexities.

After this initial assessment, see below the following steps that you may be taking:

  1. Screening: We'll contact you to confirm your fit to the position, it can be through a quick interview or chat messages)
  2. HR Interview: This is when we present the position and get to know you better
  3. Technical Interview: An opportunity to expand on the questions you answered before and better understand if you meet the position requisitions.Take the opportunity to ask your own technical questions about the project.
  4. Client interview: depending on the client, you might be invited to a client interview also, if that's the case a prep call will be held so you can better understand what the client expects from you in this stage.
  5. Offer: If you pass all the previous steps, an offer will be formally made to you.
  6. Admission: When you accept the offer, the formal admission process begins and you will receive information about how to send you documentation.

If you are passionate about data and want to make a significant impact, we encourage you to apply!

Our benefits:

-Health and dental insurance
-Meal and food allowance
-Childcare assistance
-Extended paternity leave
-Partnership with gyms and health and wellness professionals via Wellhub (Gympass) TotalPass;
-Profit Sharing and Results Participation (PLR);
-Life insurance
-Continuous learning platform (CI&T University);
-Discount club
-Free online platform dedicated to physical, mental, and overall well-being
-Pregnancy and responsible parenting course
-Partnerships with online learning platforms
-Language learning platform
And many more!
More details about our benefits here: https://ciandt.com/br/pt-br/carreiras
At CI&T, inclusion starts at the first contact. If you are a person with a disability, it is important to present your assessment during the selection process. See which data needs to be included in the report by clicking here.This way, we can ensure the support and accommodations that you deserve. If you do not yet have the assessment, don't worry: we can support you in obtaining it.
We have a dedicated Health and Well-being team, inclusion specialists, and affinity groups who will be with you at every stage. Count on us to make this journey side by side.

title: Data Gouvernance & Management : Comment maitriser et valoriser la donnée à trave
excerpt: ContexteMaîtriser et valoriser les données pour soutenir ses ambitions stratégiques est depuis plusieurs années au cœur des préoccupations de nombreuses entreprises et organisations.
description:
Contexte

Maîtriser et valoriser les données pour soutenir ses ambitions stratégiques est depuis plusieurs années au cœur des préoccupations de nombreuses entreprises et organisations. La mise en place d’une gouvernance des données en est un levier clé de maitrise et de valorisation de ces dernières mais elle ne peut être indépendante des processus et enjeux métiers.

Wavestone propose des approches simples pour sortir des logiques de gouvernance des données « hors sols » et engager une transformation Data-Driven alignant équipes Métiers, Data-Science & IT. Nous accompagnons ainsi les Directions Data (CDO) & les pôles Data des DSI dans la construction de feuilles de route de gouvernance des données pragmatiques ainsi que le pilotage de leur mise en œuvre.

Forte de plus 400 consultant.es, la practice Digital Customer de Wavestone a pour vocation d’accompagner nos clients de bout en bout sur leurs grands programmes de transformation : depuis la naissance d’une idée et sa concrétisation sous forme de design de produits & services tout ou partie digitaux, jusqu’à son opérationnalisation permettant le passage à l’échelle.​

Objectif du stage

Dans le cadre de ce stage/alternance, vous serez intégré.e activement à la communauté « Data, Analytics & IA » de la practice Digital Customer afin de participer à la formalisation de ses convictions, étoffer sa force de veille technologique, dynamiser son activité commerciale auprès des clients du cabinet, et d’aboutir à la production de supports commerciaux ou de livrables de missions.

Au sein de l’équipe Data Gouvernance, votre contribution se matérialisera dans la production d’un livrable de type livre blanc, état de l’Art, benchmark ou book de convictions sur une thématique précise à définir lors de votre arrivée avec le responsable de la communauté.

Travaux à réaliser

Afin d’enrichir l’offre Data Gouvernance & Management, vous serez assisté.e par un.e consultant.e expérimenté.e dans la réalisation d’un ou plusieurs des livrables suivants :

  • Production d’une cartographie d’acteurs, projets ou outils autour d’une technologie donnée
  • Rédaction d’un état de l’art sur un sujet émergent
  • Production d’une synthèse de la vision de Wavestone sur une thématique donnée

Une thématique précise vous sera confiée lors de votre arrivée en lien avec les besoins de l’offre. Pour information, vous pouvez trouver ci-dessous des exemples de sujets traités par d’anciens stagiaires :

  • Open Data : Construction d’un radar des initiatives Open Data & rédaction d’une synthèse de la vision Wavestone sur le sujet
  • DataMesh : Rédaction d’un état de l’art pour définir le sujet et ses principes, forger des convictions et proposer une solution d’accompagnement
  • Data for Good : Construction d’un radar des initiatives Data for Good au sein d’associations et entreprises privées & rédaction d’un livre de convictions

En parallèle, vous participerez à une ou plusieurs missions de conseil auprès de nos clients, en étant intégré dans une équipe de consultant.e.s placée sous la responsabilité d’un.e directeur.rice de mission.

Vous pourrez être amené.e à interroger des entreprises, des éditeurs, ou des start-ups et le travail réalisé pourra faire l’objet de publication(s) et être partagé au sein de la practice. 

Étudiant.e au sein d'une grande école d'ingénieurs, de management ou d'une grande université, vous êtes attiré.e par le conseil et vous possédez une forte appétence pour les sujets de transformation digitale au sein d’entreprises leaders de leur secteur.

Vous souhaitez développer des compétences liées au métier du conseil et aux problématiques de transformation des organisations.

Vos atouts :

  • Curiosité, esprit d’analyse aiguisé et capacité à vous approprier de nouveaux sujets ;
  • Orientation client, excellent relationnel et sens du service ;
  • Goût du challenge et des résultats ;
  • Attrait pour l'entreprenariat et l'innovation.

Par ailleurs, vous voulez être acteur.rice d'un projet d'entreprise ambitieux en pleine croissance en France et à l'international. Vous avez envie de mettre votre enthousiasme au service d'un cabinet qui saura vous proposer une prise de responsabilités rapide.

Wavestone est un employeur inclusif qui s'engage pour l'égalité des chances. Dans le cadre de cette politique de diversité et inclusion, Wavestone accompagne les personnes en situation de handicap et/ou nécessitant un aménagement durant leur process de recrutement et tout au long de leur prise de poste.

Wavestone est un cabinet de conseil, qui a pour mission d'accompagner les entreprises et organisations dans leurs transformations stratégiques dans un monde en constante évolution, avec l’ambition de générer des impacts positifs et durables pour toutes ses parties prenantes.

Forts de plus de 5 500 collaborateurs talentueux et engagés à travers l'Europe, l'Amérique du Nord et l'Asie, nous disposons d'expertises sectorielles de premier plan et d'un portefeuille de savoir-faire cross-sectoriels, nous permettant d'adresser à 360° les grands programmes de transformation de nos clients.

Chez Wavestone, nous sommes convaincus que la réussite professionnelle et l'épanouissement personnel vont de pair. C'est pourquoi nous offrons un environnement de travail stimulant, inclusif et bienveillant, où chacun peut s’épanouir et atteindre son plein potentiel.

Wavestone est coté sur Euronext, à Paris, et labellisé Great Place to Work®.

title: Data Owner H/F
excerpt: Votre future équipe Vous rejoignez l'équipe en charge des projets de transformation digitale sous la supervision de Loïc, responsable d'activités.
description:
Votre future équipe

Vous rejoignez l'équipe en charge des projets de transformation digitale sous la supervision de Loïc, responsable d'activités.

En tant que Data Owner, vous contribuez au développement de l'activité autour de la gestion des données et de la transformation numérique au sein du Pôle Digital Transformation Services en accompagnant les clients sur leurs projets, sur des prestations de conseil et d’expertise, de gestion et évolution de produits digitaux (applications métier) et de business intelligence, afin de fluidifier et simplifier l’usage de la donnée par les utilisateurs.

En lien avec les activités métiers, vos principales missions seront les suivantes :

  • Gouvernance de la Donnée : Contribuer activement à la définition, à l'évolution et à la mise en œuvre de la gouvernance de la donnée, en garantissant la conformité aux règles de l'entreprise et aux réglementations en matière de protection des données.
  • Gestion Centralisée des Données : Assurer la centralisation efficace de la gestion des données, en veillant à un accès facilité tout en maintenant la qualité et la sécurité requises.
  • Identification et Industrialisation des Solutions : Repérer les initiaves locales à forte valeur ajoutée et les intégrer de manière évolutive sur l'ensemble du parc, contribuant ainsi à l'optimisation des processus.
  • Création et Maintenance des Catalogues de Données : Collaborer avec l'équipe métier pour créer et maintenir des catalogues de données, répertoriant les sources de données, leur origine, leur utilisation et leurs métadonnées.
  • Qualité et Sécurité des Données : Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la sécurité des données à travers une gouvernance efficace.
  • Conduite du Changement : Participer à la mise en place de parcours de formation et de carrière évolutifs liés aux nouveaux rôles DATA, tout en répondants aux attentes data du parc.

Pourquoi rejoindre la communauté des Switchers ?

Rejoignez la communauté des Switchers et faites partie d'une aventure digitale passionnante au cœur de l'industrie nucléaire !

  • 💪 Plus de 55 ans d’expérience dans le nucléaire et positionné dans le top 3 des plus grandes entreprises d’ingénierie nucléaire.

  • 🚀 Participer à des projets stimulants avec un véritable impact sociétal.

  • 📈 De nombreuses opportunités de carrière, avec 70% de nos managers issus de la promotion interne.

Vos atouts et les prérequis pour réussir

  • De formation Ingénieur ou Bac+ 5 en data science, statistiques, mathématiques ou informatique, vous disposez d'au moins 5 ans d'expériences dans le traitement de données métier.

  • Vous avez également une connaissance des fondamentaux de la modélisation de données, et une culture BI/data.

  • Une expérience préalable dans l'un des secteurs suivants : Ingénierie, Maintenance, Exploitation, Projets, Chimie, PDR et Logistique, Sureté, Sécurité et Radioprotection, serait un atout.

Nous nous engageons au respect de l’égalité de traitement entre les candidats, et célébrons toutes les formes de diversité. Chez Assystem, seules les compétences comptent ! Si vous souhaitez porter à la connaissance d’Assystem une quelconque situation ou des besoins spécifiques, n’hésitez pas vous serez accompagné(e) !

Trouver des solutions au dérèglement climatique est la priorité du 21ème siècle, et implique de switcher à l’énergie bas-carbone. Chez Assystem, on s’est donc donné pour mission d’accélérer la transition énergétique partout dans le monde. Et pour y parvenir, nos 8 000 Switchers couplent leur expertise historique en ingénierie et en management de projet aux technologies digitales.

Présent dans 13 pays (Europe, Moyen-Orient, Asie), nous travaillons sur la production et la distribution d’électricité bas-carbone, à travers le développement des énergies nucléaires et renouvelables. Nous participons également à modernisation des réseaux électriques et l’électrification des usages, à travers l’hydrogène pour décarboner les secteurs des transports et de l’industrie.

About Atos Group

Atos Group is a global leader in digital transformation with c. 67,000 employees and annual revenue of c. €10 billion, operating in 61 countries under two brands — Atos for services and Eviden for products. The purpose of Atos Group is to help design the future of the information space. Across the world, the Group enables its customers and employees, and members of societies at large to live, work and develop sustainably, in a safe and secure information space.

About Atos Amplify

Atos Amplify provides consulting and advisory services as part of the Atos Group. Our 800 consultants across Europe, support our clients to confidently navigate their transformation journey and to embrace a digital future beyond technology. Our consultants focus on delivering measurable value, operational efficiency, and long-term impact.

We achieve this through our three core offerings: Driving Business Transformation, Unlocking the Value of Data and AI, and Maximising Potential of Platforms & Hyperscalers.

Where Purpose Meets Career

The Applied AI team within Amplify are a rapidly expanding and strategically important capability. Our offering focuses on strengthening the foundations, leveraging existing data ecosystem and capitalising on the potential of AI. You will join a dynamic, multi-functional team that solves complex client issues across both the private and public sectors, with extensive training and support available.

About the Role

We are looking for a Data Consultant with 3-4 years of experience. This is a consulting‑led role for a commercially aware data professional who works closely with clients to shape how data is governed, managed, and used to drive better decision‑making. The role suits someone who enjoys operating at the intersection of business strategy, data, and emerging AI‑driven technologies.

As a Data Consultant, you will help clients understand their data challenges, define clear data strategies and roadmaps, and translate business objectives into actionable data‑led initiatives. You will work closely with client stakeholders and Atos delivery teams to shape pragmatic, scalable solutions aligned to modern data and AI best practice.

 

Key Responsibilities

  • Data Strategy & Governance: Advise clients on data strategy, data governance, and operating models, defining how data is managed, governed, and consumed across the organisation.

  • Roadmap Creation: Support the creation of data and AI roadmaps aligned to business priorities and organisational maturity.

  • Collaboration & Teamwork: Work with architects and delivery teams to shape modern data solutions, including cloud‑based and analytics‑led platforms.

  • Stakeholder Engagement & Problem Solving: Facilitate client workshops and interviews to gather requirements, clarify business problems, and co‑create data‑led solutions.

  • Analytical thinker: Translate complex data and analytics concepts into clear, compelling narratives for senior stakeholders.

Additional Requirements

  • UK mobility with regular client‑site engagement

title: Data Engineer
excerpt: About BJAKBJAK is building the next-generation insurance and financial services platform - designed to be intuitive, intelligent and personalised.
description:
About BJAK

BJAK is building the next-generation insurance and financial services platform - designed to be intuitive, intelligent and personalised. Presently we are the largest insurance platform in Southeast Asia, and expanding globally with a strong focus on technology and product superiority. We are looking for talented, ownership-minded individuals. In return, expect growth, trust, autonomy, rewards, and impact.

About the Role

As a Data Engineer, you will build and operate the data infrastructure that powers BJAK’s analytics, product insights, and downstream systems. You will work closely with analytics, product, and engineering teams to ensure data is reliable, accessible, and production-ready at scale.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable, fault-tolerant data pipelines (batch and/or streaming) for core business and product data.

  • Ingest data from diverse sources including APIs, databases, event streams, and third-party services, ensuring high data quality and reliability.

  • Design and manage data models and storage layers (data warehouses, data lakes) that support analytics and downstream use cases.

  • Partner with analytics, product, and engineering teams to deliver clean, well-documented datasets that enable self-service analytics and experimentation.

  • Implement monitoring, logging, and alerting to ensure pipeline reliability, performance, and cost efficiency.

  • Enforce data governance best practices, including access control, privacy, documentation, and data lineage.

What You Will Need

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, data-heavy Backend Engineer, or similar role.

  • Strong programming skills in Python and/or TypeScript, with solid SQL proficiency.

  • Good understanding of data modeling, ETL/ELT concepts, and analytics workflows.

  • Hands-on experience with data warehouses (e.g. BigQuery, Snowflake).

  • Experience building and operating production data pipelines.

Nice-to-Have

  • Experience with cloud platforms (especially GCP).

  • Exposure to streaming systems (Kafka, Pub/Sub).

  • Familiarity with dbt and analytics engineering practices.

  • Experience with data lake technologies.

  • Exposure to ML data pipelines or feature stores.

Why Join BJAK

  • Build and own data systems that operate at real scale

  • Work on high-impact, business-critical data use cases

  • High-ownership role with autonomy and trust

  • Flat structure - execution and results matter more than titles

  • Competitive compensation and strong growth opportunities

title: Data Analyst
excerpt: Location: Remote, New York City, or Oakland, CA • Travel is encouraged approximately once every 6 months to state partners within the continental USA, and occasionally required.
description:
Location: Remote, New York City, or Oakland, CA

  • Travel is encouraged approximately once every 6 months to state partners within the continental USA, and occasionally required. We expect these trips to include visits to prisons and parole offices.

Role Type: Full-time

Compensation: $97,000

Visa sponsorship: Not available

Hires remotely in: United States

Experience: 2+ years of full-time, professional work experience

Recidiviz is thrilled to be hiring a Data Analyst to join our team! We’re a small, fast-moving group that works with complex criminal justice data from our state partners and develops analyses and tools that target the drivers of incarceration. That’s where you come in. We’re looking for someone who has attention to detail to improve our analysis, creativity and inquisitiveness to identify and solve open-ended problems, clarity to think through complex and variable data, flexibility to adjust in a rapidly evolving environment, and above all else, a collaborative, kind nature to add to our culture.

About us

Recidiviz is creating safer, healthier communities by improving outcomes for people in the criminal justice system. We build technology to reduce the number of people in prison and to help criminal justice leaders embrace data-driven decision-making.

Using software tools and thoughtful product design, we’ve been able to safely and permanently reduce incarceration and improve outcomes. We work side-by-side with leaders of the criminal justice system, people in prison and on supervision, and ecosystem partners to build a better path forward. In addition to the revenue we earn from state partners, some of the most respected philanthropists and foundations support our work.

People who succeed at Recidiviz lead with kindness and humility, assume good intent, learn from failure, and fix problems when they see them. They are excited to work arm in arm with government leadership and staff to improve outcomes. They are great listeners. They build deep relationships with and empathy for the people we work with and the people who our mission serves, even when those two groups feel at odds.

About the role

This role collaborates across several teams to guide work that targets the drivers of incarceration. Our Data Analysts work with diverse data – criminal justice data both raw and processed from across the country, reports and policy documents from government agencies, and much more – to develop our understanding of the criminal justice system and to motivate change. They perform analyses, contribute to tooling that improve our data and how we work with it, and help drive product launches by ensuring each product in each state has the analytical data required to drive the impact we seek. Our Data Analysts also play a key role in contextualizing and presenting their analyses to key stakeholders, both internal and external.

Travel is encouraged approximately once every 6 months to state partners within the continental USA, and occasionally required. We expect these trips to include visits to prisons and parole offices.

Core responsibilities

  • Exploring our datasets to find new insights about American criminal justice systems
  • Contextualizing and communicating key findings to internal and external stakeholders
  • Collaborating with our State Engagement and Implementation Engineering teams to design and execute on targeted roadmaps that drive impact in the states where we work
  • Collaborating with our Product Management, Data Science, and Software Engineering teams to bring analyses and data modeling into production software
  • Analyzing trends and changes in areas related to our own work to understand how our products function

Wild success

A year from now:

  • You’ve driven impact. Your work has led to several launches of our products across the country and has helped quantify the impact those tools are having on the outcomes of the justice system.

  • You’ve leveled up our analytical team. Your contributions to our tooling and processes, as well as your support for your teammates, has helped set our team up to achieve more impact sooner.

Minimum qualifications

  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, to help nurture a knowledgeable, data-oriented culture
  • Enthusiasm for working with internal and external partners from diverse backgrounds and worldviews, such as government staff, researchers, journalists, technical staff, and practitioners in criminal justice reform
  • Ability to inspect source data, reason about data models, examine results closely, and prepare data and analyses that are usable for various applications
  • 2 or more years of practical experience exploring, analyzing, and processing complex datasets
  • Professional work experience using SQL to work with large datasets, including high proficiency writing efficient queries for more complex data extraction and transformation tasks
  • High familiarity with Python, specifically Pandas package, for tasks such as statistical analysis and data visualization

Preferred qualifications

  • Experience working in a cross-functional organization that designs, builds, and launches products for users
  • Experience working with large datasets from the public sector, especially related to criminal justice
  • Familiarity with our data stack, which includes Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, Looker, Jupyter notebooks, and Python/Pandas

Compensation

Compensation is standardized based on roles and responsibilities. This ensures equitable compensation and responsible stewardship of our resources. As such, we do not negotiate compensation offers.

The annual compensation for this role is: $97,000.

What we offer

  • Effective, extremely thoughtful colleagues, working together on a mission that matters
  • 90% medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage for you and your dependents
  • Flexible time off, including 20 days of PTO, 3 days of paid volunteering time, and 13 paid holidays
  • 12 weeks of paid parental leave
  • 401(k) retirement plan with 5% company match and no vesting period
  • Complimentary One Medical membership (depending on location)
  • Partnership with Carrot to provide employees with inclusive fertility and family-forming benefits, as well as a small but growing number of hormonal health and gender-affirming care benefits
  • Monthly ClassPass credit for personal wellness
  • Free mental health support via Talkspace
  • Well-located offices in Oakland and Manhattan, with the flexibility to work remotely or go into either office as you choose
  • An annual company offsite each spring and other ad-hoc gatherings
  • All the exhilarating challenges and stretch opportunities of a tech startup, combined with the mission-driven heart of a nonprofit

More about Recidiviz

Recidiviz was an all-volunteer effort until early 2019, when Clementine, Andrew, and Joshua founded the organization. Since then, we’ve built an A-team of software engineers, designers, product managers and domain experts, from companies like Google, Apple, Dropbox, Opower, and Sidewalk Labs. Recidiviz was part of Y-Combinator’s 2019 class and has received support from some of the most respected philanthropists and foundations, including Ford Foundation, Mackenzie Scott, Schmidt Futures, Arnold Ventures, Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, and the Mozilla Foundation.

Today, Recidiviz works with (and earns revenue from) over 18 state partners – big and small, red and blue – and covers more than 40% of the US incarcerated population. We have helped to get tens of thousands of people out of the criminal justice system, safely and equitably, and saved states nearly $1 billion. In the next five years, Recidiviz plans to partner with 40 states and help 250,000 people who are stuck in the system to get out and stay out. In addition to partnering with state Departments of Corrections, Recidiviz collaborates with and learns from partners – from organizations like the Correctional Leaders Association (CLA) to community based organizations and justice-impacted individuals, whose perspectives guide our work.

To learn more about how we do what we do, check out our 2024 Year in Review, read some press coverage, or watch our TED Talk. And if you’re really keen to see our work, you can dive into our Github :).

An important note

Lasting change is always built on diversity. Recidiviz recruits, employs, trains, compensates and promotes regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, disability, age, veteran status, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, gender identity and all protected status as required by applicable law. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you have a special need that requires accommodation, please let us know in your application. Even if you don't think you meet all the criteria above, drop your resume, and we'll take a look – you might be great for another role or another time!

title: Head of Data
excerpt: Corporate:Light & Wonder’s corporate team is comprised ofincredible talent that works across the enterprise, defying boundaries to provide essential services in an extraordinary manner to ensure the success of the organization and the well-being of employees.
description:
Corporate:

Light & Wonder’s corporate team is comprised ofincredible talent that works across the enterprise, defying boundaries to provide essential services in an extraordinary manner to ensure the success of the organization and the well-being of employees.

Position Summary

The Head of Data is a critical, highly visible leadership role at the heart of Light & Wonder's AI transformation. Reporting directly to the Chief Strategy Officer, this leader will build L&W's centralized data hub, establishing the infrastructure, master data management, governance, and data science capability that the enterprise AI agenda depends on. This role partners directly with the Executive Leadership Team, business unit leaders, Finance, Operations, and Technology to ensure data becomes a defensible source of competitive advantage and a measurable driver of business value.

Light & Wonder has embarked on an ambitious AI-enabled transformation, and the Head of Data will serve as a core architect of that journey, translating L&W's data ambition into infrastructure, capability, and outcomes that directly support the enterprise AI AEBITDA agenda. This is an executive level role with significant scope, significant visibility and significant impact.

Primary Roles and Responsibilities Include:

Lead the Build of L&W's Centralized Data Function

  • Stand up L&W's centralized data hub, including infrastructure, master data management, governance, data engineering, analytics engineering, and data science capability.

  • Define and operate the hub-and-spoke operating model that pairs centralized standards with embedded data partnership across business units and functions.

  • Hire, onboard, and develop the full data hub team, building a high-performing organization with a strong talent bar and low attrition.

Develop and Execute the Multi-Year Data Strategy

  • Develop a multi-year data roadmap covering infrastructure, master data management, governance, and AI-aligned use-case sequencing, endorsed by the Executive Leadership Team and integrated into the broader AI transformation plan.

  • Translate L&W's enterprise strategy and AI ambitions into a sequenced, executable data investment plan with clear value, timing, and accountability.

  • Make architectural and platform decisions that establish Snowflake-as-source-of-truth, modern data tooling, and governance standards across the enterprise.

Drive Measurable Business Value

  • Deliver an early, fully shipped, financially-validated win that demonstrates the value of the data function, visible to leadership, signed off by Finance, and meaningful in dollar impact.

  • Stand up data infrastructure that materially supports Finance and Operations in achieving their AI AEBITDA targets, including documented data dictionaries, lineage, and shared definitions.

  • Ensure the data infrastructure built and operated by this role drives a meaningful share of L&W's enterprise AI AEBITDA target by 2028, with Finance attestation.

  • Build and deliver an R&D Intelligence Layer to support portfolio and game development decision-making, partnering closely with the Head of Product.

Establish Enterprise Data Governance and Trust

  • Establish and operate enterprise data governance, including policies, stewardship roles, data domains, decision rights, and an executive Data Council. Governance must drive measurable adherence and behavior change, not just policy documentation.

  • Ensure data quality, security, regulatory compliance, and AI-readiness across critical data domains.

  • Build the trust, transparency, and shared definitions that allow data to be confidently used in decisions across Finance, Operations, and the business units.

Executive Leadership Collaboration and Strategic Influence

  • Serve as a trusted partner to the Chief Strategy Officer, Executive Leadership Team, and Board on data and AI priorities.

  • Build coalitions with peer leaders across Technology, Finance, Operations, and the business units, driving alignment without relying on hierarchical authority.

  • Prepare and deliver executive-level communications, insights, and recommendations that translate data and AI strategy into business outcomes.

Qualifications

  • Significant experience leading data, analytics, or data engineering organizations at the VP, SVP, or Chief Data Officer level.

  • Track record of building data capability in technically complex environments, including organizations with fragmented systems, multiple ERPs, and limited integrated data infrastructure. Comfort and credibility operating in technical complexity is essential.

  • Demonstrated ability to translate data investments into measurable business value, expressed in dollars of revenue, cost, or AEBITDA, rather than platforms shipped or models built.

  • Hands-on credibility in modern data infrastructure and data engineering, including cloud data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, or equivalent), modern data tooling (dbt, MDM tooling), and ML platform fundamentals, sufficient to make architectural calls and hire well.

  • Strong working knowledge of current AI tooling, agentic workflows, LLM capabilities, and how they fit into an enterprise data strategy.

  • Proven ability to develop proof points and pilots through iterative phases, demonstrating value sequentially rather than betting on big-bang delivery.

  • Track record of building cross-functional coalitions with peer-level executives such as CTOs, CFOs, COOs, and BU heads, and navigating disagreements with peer technical leaders.

  • Demonstrated ability to attract, retain, and develop top-tier talent, with a track record of hiring people stronger than themselves and holding a high bar.

  • Public-company experience strongly preferred, including comfort with audit, regulatory, and disclosure requirements.

  • Industry experience in gaming, hospitality, entertainment, media, or another complex multi-business-unit consumer enterprise is a plus.

Education:

  • Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree (MBA, MS, PhD, or equivalent in a quantitative or technical field) preferred.

Work Conditions:

This role is open to remote work for the right individual, with a strong preference of being full time in Las Vegas, NV. If remote, 50% travel will be required.

We do not request or consider salary history, though candidates may voluntarily share pay expectations.

title: VP, Head of Data
excerpt: Be essential at Cars CommerceAt Cars Commerce, we’re fanatical about simplifying everything about car buying and selling.
description:
Be essential at Cars Commerce

At Cars Commerce, we’re fanatical about simplifying everything about car buying and selling. We do right by our customers and consumers to better connect the industry with simplified and tierless technology to enhance, measure and drive local automotive retail. Whether through our No.1 most recognized marketplace,Cars.com, our industry-leading digital experience,Dealer Inspire, our trade and appraisal technology,AccuTrade,our reputation-based digital wholesale auction marketplace, Dealerclub,or our newCars Commerce Media Network, Cars Commerce is essential for success in the automotive industry.

No one ever travels alone here: at its core, Cars Commerce is collaboration. In fact, it’s built into the very fabric of our shared values. We like to say weRise Together– putting people at the center of what we do, from consumer to customer to community. Life at Cars Commerce makes it easy when we share the ethos to beOpen to All, encouraging open-minded communication because we know diverse thinking yields better outcomes. But critical to our success isCaring to ChallengeandTaking Ownership, fueling a competitive spirit in a respectful environment where we think about tomorrow but act today. At our foundation, we have integrity,Doing the Right Thing, even when it’s hard. It’s our shared commitment to these values that makes Cars Commerce a place where growth becomes not only possible, but downright unavoidable.

But don’t take our word for it. As a U.S. News & World ReportBest Company to Work For in 2024, we're obsessive about the employee experience. We are among the top 20% being declared “Best” of our industry based on six critical factors that are important to employee wellbeing, like quality of pay, benefits, work life balance and more.

The Role
We are seeking a strategic and execution-oriented Head of Data to lead our enterprise data organization through its next stage of modernization and integration.

Reporting to the CTO and partnering closely with the CEO and Executive Leadership Team, the Head of Data will unify Data Engineering, Data Science, Revenue Analytics, and Business Intelligence into a cohesive, high-performing organization. This leader will drive architectural stabilization, governance clarity, and improved decision velocity across the company.

This is a transformation mandate grounded in pragmatic execution: stabilizing and rationalizing a fragmented data environment while delivering measurable business value along the way.

The Head of Data serves as the executive integrator across data pillars, aligning technical architecture with business priorities.

What You’ll Own

  • Enterprise Data Strategy Governance and Risk

  • Establish a trusted single source of truth for core enterprise metrics

  • Define and execute a multi-year enterprise data roadmap spanning engineering, analytics, and science

  • Align data capabilities to support marketplace growth, revenue expansion, and operational excellence

  • Accountable for defining and enforcing enterprise-wide standards for data definitions, metric integrity, and stewardship across all business functions

  • Own the identification and mitigation of enterprise data risks, including metric inconsistency, governance gaps, architectural fragility, and compliance exposure

  • Establish clarity across departments on trusted performance indicators

Data Architectural Stabilization and Modernization

  • Execute a multi-year modernization plan to democratize data consumption across the company with a high degree of confidence behind the metrics

  • Deliver meaningful business utility within the first 6 months while driving long-term architectural integrity

  • Rationalize a highly fragmented legacy data schema into scalable, domain-oriented models with clear sources of truth

  • Establish instrumentation and data contracts that enforce trust, and support near real-time experimentation and optimization

  • Establish engineering excellence standards that improve data reliability observability, and deployment rigor across the platform.

  • Establish architectural foundations capable of supporting event-driven intelligence and AI readiness

Revenue Acceleration & Decision Velocity

  • Drive the evolution from static reporting to near real-time, decision-ready intelligence across marketplace performance, revenue operations, and executive KPIs

  • Transition data from a reporting function to a revenue-driving capability by building trust around metrics and quality drivers that ensure accuracy

  • Improve time-to-insight for revenue, dealership performance, and marketplace KPIs

  • Enable reliable forecasting, pricing optimization, and experimentation frameworks

  • Advise the CEO and Executive Leadership Team on data-related capital allocation, technology investments, and risk tradeoffs, ensuring disciplined ROI and long-term platform sustainability

  • Organizational Leadership

  • Enable a “culture of acceleration” across the Cars Commerce business that increases velocity and impact over time through democratized, trusted data

  • Integrate and align multidisciplinary data functions under a unified operating model

  • Build an “accelerating organization” that increases delivery velocity and technical rigor over time

  • Serve as executive sponsor for data-related investment strategy, vendor partnerships, and cloud spend optimization

  • Recruit, develop, and retain top-tier data leadership talent

  • Data Science & Advanced Analytics

  • Oversee applied data science initiatives including predictive modeling, optimization, and experimentation

  • Ensure models integrate directly into product and revenue workflows

  • Balance long-term AI readiness with practical business impact

Governance & Trust

  • Establish pragmatic data governance and stewardship frameworks, working across the enterprise to ensure coordination with appropriate business, legal, information security and finance partners

  • Ensure data accuracy, availability, security, and compliance

  • Drive clarity on metric ownership and accountability across the enterprise

Who You Are
You are a seasoned data executive who operates as both strategist and practitioner. You have led multidisciplinary data organizations at scale and understand how to modernize legacy environments without disrupting business continuity. You treat data as a business function, not just a technical one.

You bring the mindset of an engineer, grounded in systems thinking, architectural integrity, and disciplined execution, even as you operate at the highest levels of enterprise leadership.

Qualifications

  • 15+ years of progressive data leadership experience, with 5+ years at the VP/SVP level leading multidisciplinary organizations across data engineering, analytics, and data science

  • Demonstrated history influencing C-suite leaders and public company Boards to secure investment and deliver enterprise-scale data transformation initiatives

  • Architectural & Data Foundations

  • Strong foundational experience in database architecture, data engineering, or data platform design, with the ability to deeply understand data pipelines, schema design, and system-level data integrity

  • Demonstrated ability to steer and evaluate enterprise data platform decisions across storage, compute, orchestration, and semantic modeling, prioritizing long-term architectural integrity over tool familiarity

  • Proven track record guiding teams toward trusted, high-integrity data pipelines and “single source of truth” environments

Technical Leadership vs Tool Expertise

  • Experience selecting and evolving data stack components based on business needs and scalability requirements, without being dependent on any single vendor ecosystem

  • Comfort engaging in detailed architectural reviews while maintaining executive-level perspective

AI & Intelligent Systems Literacy

  • Working understanding of the systems, data infrastructure, and experimentation frameworks required to enable scalable AI/ML initiatives

  • Experience supporting rapid model experimentation and productionization environments, without needing to serve as a hands-on modeler

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent practical experience

  • Advanced degree (e.g., MS, MBA) is a plus but not required

What Success Looks Like
First 6 Months

  • Conduct rapid enterprise data assessment across architecture, governance, and operating model

  • Deliver a prioritized modernization roadmap with clear sequencing and investment implications

  • Establish clarity and accountability for core enterprise metrics

  • Begin the process of transformation to increase trust in data by Implementing 1–2 high-impact automation or instrumentation initiatives around key business metrics

Year 1

  • Northstar continues to be to Improve reliability and trust in enterprise performance metrics

  • Institutionalize enterprise data usage by embedding trusted metrics into planning cycles, performance management, forecasting, and cross-functional decision making processes.

  • Stabilize and rationalize foundational data architecture to reduce fragmentation and technical debt

  • Core business dashboards operate on near real-time (at worst daily) refresh cycles with clearly defined metric ownership and trust

  • Increased adoption of self-service analytics

  • Eliminate manual reporting dependencies on key reports and increase workflow automation

  • Advance data democratization across business functions

Year 2+

  • Continue to strengthen the culture of acceleration in the company through easily accessible business insights (including lightweight dashboarding self-service interfaces and natural language data access).

  • Modern, scalable data platform recognized as a competitive advantage

  • Predictive intelligence embedded in product and revenue workflows

  • High-performing data organization operating with clarity and velocity

Why This Role Matters
This is a rare opportunity to shape the enterprise data foundation of a scaled digital marketplace at an inflection point. This leader will help define how enterprise performance is measured, governed, and translated into strategic action.

This role sits at the intersection of technology, finance, product, and revenue. The Head of Data will influence how the company defines performance, allocates capital, manages risk, and leverages data as a strategic asset.

Success requires executive presence, the ability to influence and align senior leaders around enterprise standards, and the discipline to translate architectural complexity into strategic clarity without disrupting the business.

In addition to base salary, some roles are eligible for our bonus and/or equity programs, depending on level and role. Regular full-time positions are eligible for our comprehensive benefits package. If you are hired at Cars Commerce, your final base salary compensation will be determined based on factors such as skills and/or experience. title: Senior Data Governance Analyst
excerpt: Type and Estimated Length of Employment: Fixed term.
description:
Type and Estimated Length of Employment: Fixed term. 18 months

About Water.org

1 in 4 people around the world lack access to safe water and 2 in 5 people don’t have access to a safe toilet. We are working every day to change this. We are Water.org. We’re here to bring water and sanitation to the world. We want to make it safe, accessible, and cost-effective because we believe that water is the way to empower a bright future that we can all share in, worldwide. We are an international nonprofit organization based in Kansas City, Missouri that has positively transformed millions of lives around the world through access to safe water and sanitation. Founded by Gary White and Matt Damon, we pioneer market-driven financial solutions to the global water crisis. For more than 30 years, we’ve been providing women hope, children health and families a bright future.

We are looking for a Senior Data Governance Analyst to join our team of thinkers and doers

If you are passionate about data governance, collaborating with others and strategizing for the future we would love to hear from you. The Data Governance Analyst is primarily responsible for the development and execution of a responsive, org-wide data governance framework that supports the full data lifecycle. The incumbent in this position will partner effectively across teams and functions to implement policies, ensure data quality and compliance, and promote consistent data practices.

What You’ll do

Data Governance
Leads implementation of the data governance operating model, managing data and change requests across the organization. Implements and maintains data governance policies, best practices, and data quality standards. Develops and oversees data stewardship processes to ensure high-quality, well-documented datasets. Ensures the scope of data governance initiatives align with enterprise strategy and priorities.

Technical + Advisory Support
Works collaboratively to define policies and ensure regulatory and organizational compliance. Provides training and support to teams on data governance practices and data management protocols. Builds data governance methodologies, incorporating enterprise and legal requirements for data privacy and protection.

Process Management + Documentation
Collaborates with internal analysts and teams to ensure governed and reliable data assets without owning analytics directly. Acts as a liaison across departments to translate data needs and collaboratively define requirements that ensure processes work across systems. Supports enterprise-wide efforts to align metadata, data lineage, and data sharing processes.

Why Water.org?

At Water.org, we’re dedicated to breaking barriers to safe water access for everyone, everywhere. When you join us, you’ll be part of a purpose-driven team committed to sustainability, diversity, and making a lasting impact. We offer growth opportunities, a collaborative culture, and the chance to drive global change.

Discover What Makes Water.org a Great Place to Work

Requirements

What Makes you a great fit:

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, information management, or related fields and/or equivalent related work experience.
  • Seven or more years of progressive responsibility with data management, including four or more years of experience with data governance management and related disciplines such as metadata management, data quality, and/or data stewardship.
  • Demonstrated knowledge with data cataloging, data quality management, and data lineage.
  • Proficient (written and verbal) in English.   

What Makes you stand out:

Demonstrated experience implementing, and managing an organizational data governance program, including the development of policies, procedures, and frameworks to ensure data quality, integrity, privacy, and compliance across systems and departments.

Travel Requirements

  • Up to 5% of domestic travel required.

Benefits

Being authorized to work in the country posted is a precondition of employment.

Type and Estimated Length of Employment: Fixed term. 18 months

Salary/Benefits

This position offers a base salary range of $85,000-$100,000. Base salary may be flexible based on skills, certifications, location and/or qualifications. In addition, this position offers participation in benefits programs provided by Water.org for the benefit of its employees if you meet the eligibility criteria. Based on the planned schedule for this role, currently, these benefits include paid time off, holidays, medical, dental, vision, life, long and short-term disability and our retirement program. This position is ineligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

description:

Empower your impact at Cision. Be seen, be understood, be you.

About the Role

BulletinHealthcare, a subsidiary of Cision, is seeking a Data Insights Manager to support our healthcare advertising and analytics platform. This role sits within our Advanced Analytics team and reports to the Director of Advanced Analytics.

BulletinHealthcare is partnered with top medical associations to bring their members essential daily updates impacting their practice. Each morning, we deliver digital briefings to over 1 million verified physicians, PharmDs, NPs, PAs, and caregivers. Each briefing is uniquely crafted for the specialties they reach.

The Data Insights Manager will work across a wide range of analytics, data engineering, and data science initiatives. The ideal candidate would own projects, moving between different types of work and delivering actionable insights. In one day, you might finetune a complex ETL pipeline and also provide a consultation to a sales rep trying to identify a specific HCP audience for a client.

This is an individual contributor role with growth potential, offering significant autonomy and the opportunity to create data products in a collaborative environment.

What You'll Do

Design and maintain data pipelines and ETL workflows that support analytics, reporting, and advertising operations

Analyze large datasets to generate actionable insights for internal teams, data partners, and advertising clients

Translate business questions into data analyses, models, and dashboards

Support and improve the systems used to measure campaign performance, audience targeting, and engagement

Work with partner data sources and internal systems to integrate and process complex datasets

Deliver both one-off analyses and long-term infrastructure projects, balancing multiple priorities across teams

Collaborate with engineering, product, operations, and client-facing teams to solve data problems and deliver measurable impact

Contribute to the development of new analytics capabilities and data products

Tools & Technologies

Our team uses the best tool for the job. Common technologies include R, Python, and MySQL.

Experience with statistical programming and data manipulation is essential; familiarity with the specific tools above is helpful but not required if you have strong experience with comparable technologies.

Qualifications

5–10 years of experience in data analytics, data science, data engineering, or related fields

Strong background in statistical programming and data analysis

Ability to own projects independently and deliver results without constant oversight

Experience working with large datasets and building data pipelines or analytical workflows

Proficiency in SQL and data manipulation concepts

Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to move effectively between different types of projects

Great communication skills

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working with healthcare data or regulated datasets

Familiarity with the privacy and compliance considerations surrounding sensitive and personally identifiable data

Experience supporting advertising analytics, marketing analytics, or media measurement

Experience working with data from external partners or multiple integrated sources

Why Join BulletinHealthcare

Work with real-world healthcare audiences

Join a small, collaborative analytics team with significant ownership of projects

Build tools and insights that influence HCP behavior

Enjoy the flexibility of a fully remote role with the opportunity to grow into broader leadership responsibilities

What You'll Do

What You'll Bring

What We Offer

As a global leader in PR, marketing and social media management technology and intelligence, Cision helps brands and organizations to identify, connect and engage with customers and stakeholders to drive business results. PR Newswire, a network of over 1.1 billion influencers, in-depth monitoring, analytics and its Brandwatch and Falcon.io social media platforms headline a premier suite of solutions. title: Data Analyst
excerpt: City Cast is the leading network of local podcasts and newsletters, with daily shows in cities across the country.
description:
City Cast is the leading network of local podcasts and newsletters, with daily shows in cities across the country. We're looking for a Data Analyst to become the connective tissue between our audience, our editorial work, and our business.

As City Cast's Data Analyst, you will be the single source of truth for our audience and revenue data. You'll build and maintain dashboards that bring together listenership, newsletter engagement, web traffic, advertising performance, and subscriber data. You'll forecast where we're headed, surface the patterns behind growth (and decline), and help our staff understand what the numbers are telling us.

This role reports into the Chief Operating Officer and works cross-functionally with editorial, revenue, marketing, and operations.

Deadline to apply: May 28, 2026

What You'll Do

  • Own City Cast's audience and revenue data end-to-end — from ingestion and cleaning through dashboarding, reporting, and storytelling.
  • Define and steward the metrics that matter to City Cast, working with leadership to align on definitions, benchmarks, and goals.
  • Work with operations, membership, revenue, finance, editorial teams to build out a robust data ecosystem.
  • Wrangle and maintain a unified analytics dashboards that pulls from podcast platforms (Megaphone, Apple Podcasts, Spotify), newsletter ESP, web analytics, our CRM, and subscription systems.
  • Design dashboards tailored to different audiences — high-level health metrics for the executive team, market-level performance for editorial, pipeline and pacing for revenue.
  • Forecast audience growth across our network, by city and by line of business, and stress-test those forecasts against real performance. Support finance and sales forecasts.
  • Develop first-party data strategy and maintenance across platforms.
  • Deliver clear, regular insights to staff — what's working, what isn't, what changed this month, and why it matters — through written reports, presentations, and informal coaching.
  • Use AI tools to scale your work — from accelerating dashboard development and data pipeline buildout, to automating routine reporting, to surfacing patterns and anomalies you'd otherwise miss. Help the broader team get more out of our data through AI-assisted workflows.
  • Support specific investigations and decisions — pricing changes, new market launches, format experiments, ad product performance — with rigorous, on-demand analysis.

Requirements

What You Bring

  • You have 4+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or audience analytics role, ideally in news, podcasting, or media.
  • You've worked in a media environment and understand the rhythms of a newsroom, the realities of audience growth, and the dynamics of advertising and subscription revenue.
  • You're fluent in SQL and comfortable in at least one BI or visualization tool (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Mode, Metabase, or similar). Experience with Python or R for analysis is a plus.
  • You're fluent with AI tools and excited to use them in your work — whether that's coding faster with an AI assistant, querying data conversationally, prototyping dashboards, or building lightweight internal tools. You have a clear-eyed sense of where AI accelerates the work and where it needs human judgment.
  • You've built dashboards that people actually use — not just look at once — and you understand the difference.
  • You're a strong communicator who can present findings to non-technical colleagues — including journalists and executives — with clarity and judgment about what matters.
  • You're self-directed and comfortable being the first and only person in this seat. You enjoy building structures where there aren't any yet.
  • You care about local journalism and the communities City Cast serves.

Benefits

This position is full-time and remote, with excellent benefits. The salary range is $80,000-$100,000 depending on experience. City Cast is an equal opportunity employer committed to excellence through diversity, which involves attracting talented people from diverse backgrounds and traditions. Read about our mission and core values here. We encourage everyone to apply.

About City Cast: We’re a network of one-of-a-kind, daily local news podcasts in cities around the country, accompanied by a daily email newsletter and website that keeps you in the know about what’s happening in your community. Currently available in Chicago, DC, Denver, Las Vegas, Madison, Nashville, Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, Portland, Salt Lake, Seattle, and Twin Cities, City Cast is the smartest, easiest way to connect with the place you live and love.

City Cast is an affiliate of Graham Holdings. Read the Graham Holdings Company California Worker Privacy Statement here.

description:
Role Purpose

The Group Data Enablement Lead activates Guardian’s enterprise data management, governance, and data product practices across the Group business. The role ensures that data from source systems, platforms, analytics, and AI use cases is trusted, well-governed, and ready for use by design. It also leads and influences stakeholders by turning enterprise data strategy and standards into practical execution and building sustainable accountability within the business over time.

Key Responsibilities

  • Operationalize enterprise data management, data governance, and AI‑readiness practices within Group value streams.

  • Partner with Group business leaders toidentify, frame, and prioritize high‑value business and AI use cases, translating them into clear data product requirements.

  • Embed data product practices into Group execution, including clear ownership, lifecycle management, quality, semantics, lineage, and consumption readiness.

  • Translate enterprise data strategy, policies, standards, and operating model expectations into actionable Group data roadmaps, delivery plans, and priorities aligned to business outcomes and Tech roadmaps.

  • Resolve cross‑stream data dependencies and tradeoffsimpactingtrust, delivery timelines, and reuse.

  • Ensure upstream data supports analytics, BI, applications, and AI use cases by design, not through after‑the‑fact remediation.

  • Partner with Platforms to embed governance, quality, and protection capabilities into Group workflows and pipelines.

  • Communicate data strategy, priorities, risks, and tradeoffs to Group leadership in clear, outcome‑oriented terms to enable informed decision‑making.

  • Track and report progress andrealizedvalue of Group data initiatives, including impact to business use cases, analytics adoption, AI readiness, and audit outcomes.

  • Serve as the primary escalation point for Group data management and governance execution issues.

Requirements

  • Demonstrated experience translating business strategy and use cases into data product requirements and execution plans.

  • Strong understanding of data product practices including ownership, lifecycle, quality, semantics, and consumption readiness.

  • Ability to partner with business leaders toidentifyhigh‑value data and AI use cases aligned to business outcomes.

  • Experience resolving cross‑team data dependencies and tradeoffs across business, analytics, and technology teams.

  • Strong business and executive communication skills, including the ability to explain data‑related tradeoffs, risks, and value clearly.

  • Comfort engaging senior business, technology, and governance leaders as a trusted partner.

Preferred Qualifications

title: Data Governance Lead
excerpt: FunctionITOur CompanyWe’re Hitachi Vantara, the data foundation trusted by the world’s innovators.
description:
Function

IT

Our Company

We’re Hitachi Vantara, the data foundation trusted by the world’s innovators. Our resilient, high-performance data infrastructure means that customers – from banks to theme parks ­– can focus on achieving the incredible with data.

If you’ve seen the Las Vegas Sphere, you’ve seen just one example of how we empower businesses to automate, optimize, innovate – and wow their customers. Right now, we’re laying the foundation for our next wave of growth. We’re looking for people who love being part of a diverse, global team – and who get excited about making a real-world impact with data.

Job description

The Company

Hitachi Vantara combines technology, intellectual property, and industry knowledge to deliver data-managing solutions that help enterprises improve their customers’ experiences, develop new revenue streams, and lower the costs of business. Hitachi Vantara elevates your innovation advantage by combining IT, operational technology (OT) and domain expertise. Come join our team and our employee-focused culture and help drive our customers’ data to meaningful customer outcomes.

The Team

Hitachi Vantara is looking for a knowledgeable data governance professional to continue maturing the existing data governance. The role focuses on deeper engagement with business partners and developing the existing Enterprise Data Governance and Master Data Management team. This role will help fully implement a data quality initiative and work closely with other data teams to support and build the data catalog and data quality tools. This role works collaboratively with several teams, including Legal, Privacy, and Cybersecurity to ensure an enterprise approach to data throughout the organization. The role relies on leveraging your understanding of an organic approach to data governance in areas of: data definition, data lineage, data quality, and data security, as well as contributing to data literacy across the organization.

In this role you will continue the work we have begun with our business areas to build out the business glossaries and data catalog, develop a process for the approval of technology standards, write data governance policies, oversee the further adoption of data governance efforts, and partner with the legal and information security teams to fulfill Hitachi Vantara commitments related to reducing company’s Data Management risks.

As the role requires close collaboration with senior-level executives (members of DG Councils/C-Suite), business data owners, and stewards, it is a must-have to be an excellent communicator who can drive the process to govern data at enterprise scale.

The Role

Support the implementation of the Data Governance strategy:

  • Partner with the Data Privacy function to support compliance with all applicable data privacy regulations and policies.
  • Collaborates with the Business Operations, Cybersecurity, and IT to identify, recommend and/or implement processes and technologies that support successful deployment of the data governance strategy across the enterprise.
  • Align tactical initiatives to the data governance strategy across projects and programs.
  • Implement data governance strategy, controls, and capabilities including data catalog, metadata management, data quality certification, data quality metrics, reference data management, data domain hierarchy and taxonomy management, reporting and analytics governance, data classification and tagging, data architecture, and data modeling
  • Support Master Data Management in keeping current service levels and enhancing MDM capabilities
  • Enhance the data literacy program to educate executives and functional teams on importance of data and drive a data driven organizational culture
  • Support the execution of the data governance vision, mission, strategy, and charter.

Lead the definition and documentation of Data Governance processes:

  • Creates or supports the creation of data and system flow documentation and process maps. Work to align other key data capabilities including metadata repositories, data dictionaries, data lineage, Business Process Maps and training materials with the data and system process flows.
  • Lead working sessions with operational and information system owners on the onboarding of new data assets including documentation of processes, procedures, system interfaces, data dictionaries, data models and requirements for all new analytical data assets.
  • Identifies data quality “hot spots” or points of exposure. Determines strategies to improve data quality controls and monitoring routines to de-risk points of data quality vulnerabilities using approved tools and solutions.
  • Collaborate with the data lake development team to implement guidelines and controls on data.

Drive Data Governance on an on-going basis:

  • Chair Data Governance Steering Committee responsible for defining data policies and standards.
  • Manage stakeholder communication and progress reporting at various levels of the organization including leadership and executives
  • Continuously identifies or supports opportunities to streamline and simplify the execution of Data Transformation initiatives and data capabilities Support the organization’s efforts around master data management, metadata management, data stewardship, data privacy and security.
  • Actively contribute with a broad range of stakeholders as part of the Data Governance Council
  • Continue the Data Governance framework including determining accountabilities, progress dashboards, published minutes, decision logs, change controls, data issue management and policies.

Operate, Monitor and Report on Data Quality Data Security:

  • Lead the design, build, and execution of data quality standards, data protection standards and adoption requirements across the enterprise.
  • Take accountability for setting documentation and enforcement policies and procedures that track and ensure high data quality is created and management in alignment with business needs.
  • Lead the creation of defining metrics and KPIs as part of a Data Quality Scorecard as well as dashboards and reports to keep track of these metrics and KPIs.
  • Forge strong working relationships with business partners to analyze root cause for data defects and duplicate data issues - prioritizing remediation efforts and ensure assessment of remediation.
  • Assist in the implementation of data security measures including ensuring sensitive data is masked for non-authorized users
  • Act as a liaison to the business on the quality of data and outstanding gaps.
  • The Data Governance Lead Analyst plays a key execution and enablement role within the Enterprise Data Governance program. This position supports the design, implementation, and day-to-day operation of data governance practices across the organization, working closely with Data Stewards, business partners, and the Enterprise Data Governance leadership team to improve data quality, consistency, usability, and trust.

Scope & Impact

  • Operational execution of enterprise data governance practices

As a Senior Data Analyst, Risk Adjustment, you will:

  • Own the claim and supplemental submission workflow for Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Encounter Data System (EDS) submissions.

  • Future success: You will serve as a trusted technical partner to Risk Adjustment and Engineering, continuously optimize encounter data pipelines and controls, ensure high-quality and compliant CMS submissions, and drive scalable solutions that support accurate risk adjustment performance and business growth.
